title: illusory-face-eeg
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings and behavioral data from 300 face, illusory face, and matched non-face object stimuli presented during three tasks: spontaneous dissimilarity judgments, face-likeness ratings, and face/object discrimination. Neural activity was recorded while participants performed an orthogonal target detection task during stimulus presentation at 3.75 Hz, enabling investigation of the neural correlates underlying distinct stages of spontaneous face perception.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during presentation of 300 images (human faces, illusory faces, and matched non-face objects) displayed centrally at 3.75 Hz. Participants completed three behavioral tasks: a triplet odd-one-out dissimilarity task, face-likeness ratings on a 0-10 scale, and a speeded face/object categorization task, while simultaneously performing an orthogonal target detection task during EEG recording.
